name: rAthena NPCs and Scripts Knowledge Pack
description: Knowledge about NPC script syntax, positioning, duplicate names, shop scripts, and client-side registrations.

entries.title: NPC Script Header Syntax
entries.summary: Standard layout of an rAthena NPC script header.
entries.details: An NPC script header is tab-separated: 'map,x,y,dir<TAB>script<TAB>NPC Name<TAB>SpriteID,{...}'. Standard types include 'script' for interactive NPCs, 'shop' for sellers, 'warp' for portals, and 'duplicate' for referencing another script with a different sprite/location.
warnings: Using spaces instead of tab characters in the header will throw a critical parser error on server start.

entries.title: Duplicate NPC Names
entries.summary: How rAthena handles duplicate NPC names and unique display names.
entries.details: All NPC display names in rAthena must be unique unless they use the name-hiding syntax. By adding '#' followed by a unique suffix (e.g. 'John#01' or 'John#02'), the client will only display 'John' to the players, while the server retains unique identifiers. Duplicate names without a '#' suffix cause database collisions.
warnings: Duplicate script NPC names without hashtags will cause compilation errors or script overrides during dry-run validation.
